title: Managed Serverless API Hosting on Vercel
description: Deploy APIs and serverless functions on Vercel’s edge-distributed platform with zero-config deployments and automatic global CDN. Serverless-only model: no persistent processes; functions have a maximum execution duration per plan tier. First-class support for Next.js including API routes, Edge Functions, and ISR.

cost.costNotes: Free Hobby tier: 100GB bandwidth/month, 100GB-hours serverless execution, unlimited deployments. Pro plan ($20/month per member) for teams, longer function timeouts (60s), and higher limits.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Min $0 represents Hobby tier usage adequate for low-traffic APIs. Max $80 represents a small team on Vercel Pro ($20/month per member) with moderate traffic.
provenance.adoptionCost: Very low adoption cost. Vercel has zero-config deployments from Git. ~2 hours setup at $100/hour = $200.
provenance.licenseCost: No platform fees for Hobby tier. Pro plan is $20/month per team member.

provides.reasoning: Vercel Serverless Functions and Edge Functions provide managed execution of API logic without server management.
Core Vercel offering: automatic scaling serverless functions invoked per request with no idle costs.
Vercel Edge Functions execute at the CDN edge with sub-millisecond cold starts for latency-sensitive logic.
Vercel's global edge network serves API responses from the closest point of presence.
Automatic deployments triggered by Git push with preview deployments per pull request.
Zero server management, automatic TLS, built-in observability, and Git-based workflows eliminate most operational overhead.

requires.reasoning: Vercel's deployment model is fundamentally Git-based (GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket).
Serverless functions are stateless and ephemeral; all persistent state must use external services.

supports_nfr.reason: Vercel's serverless architecture with global edge replication can achieve 99.9% availability; Hobby/Pro tiers lack enterprise SLA for higher targets.
Serverless function cold starts introduce minimum 50ms p95 latency; Edge Functions are faster (~10ms) but have limited APIs.
Serverless cold start variability and resource contention result in p99 latency floor of ~100ms for Node.js functions.
Vercel supports instant rollback to previous deployments via dashboard or CLI, enabling RTO as low as 1 minute.
Serverless auto-scaling handles high read QPS but Hobby tier bandwidth caps constrain cost-effective operation to ~1000 QPS.
Serverless function invocations for write operations scale automatically but function concurrency caps effective write QPS to ~500.
GDPR-compliant deployments achievable via EU region selection and appropriate data handling; Vercel Pro/Enterprise supports data residency.
CCPA compliance is an application-level concern; Vercel hosting does not constrain it.
HIPAA BAA available on Vercel Enterprise; not available on Hobby/Pro. Applications on Enterprise can be HIPAA-eligible.
SOX compliance achievable through appropriate audit logging and access controls at application level on Vercel.

supports_constraints.reason: Vercel is a cloud-agnostic SaaS platform abstracting underlying infrastructure.
Vercel supports serverless functions in Node.js/TypeScript (first-class), Python, Go, and Ruby.

Vercel hosts web applications, API endpoints, and mobile app backends via serverless functions; not suitable for batch jobs, long-running workers, or desktop/IoT backends requiring persistent connections.
Pattern specifically requires Vercel as the API/compute hosting provider.
Vercel supports edge caching and ISR for response caching, but pattern works with or without application-level caching.
Multi-tenancy is supported at application level; Vercel hosting is agnostic to single or multi-tenant architecture.
Vercel serverless functions have strict execution time limits (10s Hobby, 60s Pro) incompatible with batch processing workloads.
Serverless stateless execution model does not support distributed transaction coordination requiring persistent connections.
Vercel serverless functions do not support persistent WebSocket connections or long-lived streaming on Hobby/Pro tiers.
Vercel can host lightweight AI inference via serverless API routes calling external AI providers.
Vector search via external providers (Pinecone, Supabase) can be called from Vercel API routes.
Document stores are external services; Vercel API routes can connect to any external document database.
Vercel KV (Upstash Redis) is available as an add-on; external Redis services also supported.
Graph databases are external services; Vercel serverless functions can connect to any external graph database.
Time-series databases are external services callable from Vercel API routes.
OLTP workloads are supported via external transactional databases called from serverless functions.
Analytical workloads requiring long-running queries exceed serverless function timeout limits.
Async messaging via external queues can trigger Vercel serverless functions via webhooks or polling.

provider.description: PaaS provider for managed serverless hosting

plan_tier.description: Vercel plan tier: hobby (free, 10s function timeout), pro ($20/member/month, 60s timeout), enterprise (custom, HIPAA BAA available)

function_runtime.description: Serverless function runtime. Node.js has first-class support. Edge runtime executes at CDN edge with faster cold starts but limited APIs.

edge_functions.description: Enable Edge Functions for middleware at CDN edge. Best for auth checks, redirects, A/B testing.

enable_preview_deployments.description: Create preview deployments for every pull request with unique URLs.

warn_nfr.reason: Vercel Hobby tier bandwidth (100GB/month) and serverless execution limits may be exhausted at sustained high read QPS.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas--vercel: peak read QPS is {actual} req/s (>500). Hobby tier resource limits may be exhausted mid-month; consider Vercel Pro or dedicated infrastructure.

warn_nfr.reason: High write QPS via serverless functions is cost-inefficient due to per-invocation billing.
warn_nfr.message: hosting-managed-paas--vercel: peak write QPS is {actual} req/s (>200). Serverless per-invocation costs may exceed always-on PaaS at this write throughput.
